**Stal Ostrów Wielkopolski**, also known as  **Tasomix Rosiek Stal Ostrów Wielkopolski** for sponsorship reasons, is a Polish professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") team, based in [Ostrów Wielkopolski](/wiki/Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski "Ostrów Wielkopolski"). They play in the [Polish Basketball League](/wiki/Polish_Basketball_League "Polish Basketball League") (PLK) since its promotion back in 2015. The club won the Polish national championship in [2021](/wiki/2020%E2%80%9321_PLK_season "2020–21 PLK season") and the [Polish Cup](/wiki/Polish_Basketball_Cup "Polish Basketball Cup") in 2019 and 2022. Since then, Stal has also been active at the European stage in the [Basketball Champions League](/wiki/Basketball_Champions_League "Basketball Champions League") and [FIBA Europe Cup](/wiki/FIBA_Europe_Cup "FIBA Europe Cup").

The home arena of the team is the [Arena Ostrów](/wiki/Arena_Ostr%C3%B3w "Arena Ostrów").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/d/d1/Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski_logo.png)](/wiki/File:Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski_logo.png)

The original, non-sponsored club logo

In 2015, Stal [promoted](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") from the second-tier [I Liga](/wiki/I_Liga_(basketball) "I Liga (basketball)") to the [PLK](/wiki/Polish_Basketball_League "Polish Basketball League"). In its first season, the team finished 13th in the standings with a 12–20 record. In the 2016–17 season, Stal had a historic season as the team reached the semi-finals of the PLK after defeating [MKS Dąbrowa Górnicza](/wiki/MKS_D%C4%85browa_G%C3%B3rnicza_(basketball) "MKS Dąbrowa Górnicza (basketball)") 3–0 in the quarter-finals. In the semi-finals, Stal lost to [Stelmet Zielona Góra](/wiki/Stelmet_Zielona_G%C3%B3ra "Stelmet Zielona Góra") but won the third place series against [Energa Czarni Słupsk](/wiki/Energa_Czarni_S%C5%82upsk "Energa Czarni Słupsk").

In the 2017–18 season, Stal had an even more successful season as the team reached the PLK finals for the first time after defeating [Polski Cukier Toruń](/wiki/Polski_Cukier_Toru%C5%84 "Polski Cukier Toruń"). In the Finals, the team lost to [Anwil Włocławek](/wiki/Anwil_W%C5%82oc%C5%82awek "Anwil Włocławek"), 2–4.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4d/BM_SLAM_Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski.jpg/250px-BM_SLAM_Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski.jpg)](/wiki/File:BM_SLAM_Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski.jpg)

Stal team in 2019

In [2019](/wiki/2019_Polish_Basketball_Cup "2019 Polish Basketball Cup"), Stal won the [Polish Basketball Cup](/wiki/Polish_Basketball_Cup "Polish Basketball Cup") after defeating [Arka Gdynia](/wiki/Arka_Gdynia "Arka Gdynia") 77–74 in the final.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was the first ever trophy for the club.

In the 2020–21 season, Stal played in the [FIBA Europe Cup](/wiki/2020%E2%80%9321_FIBA_Europe_Cup "2020–21 FIBA Europe Cup") and reached the Final Four of the tournament. It reached the finals were it lost to Israeli club [Ironi Ness Ziona](/wiki/Ironi_Ness_Ziona_B.C. "Ironi Ness Ziona B.C."). Later that season, Stal won the finals of the [2020–21 PLK season](/wiki/2020%E2%80%9321_PLK_season "2020–21 PLK season"), winning its first Polish championship in club history.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Guard Jakub Garbacz was named the [PLK Finals MVP](/wiki/PLK_Finals_MVP "PLK Finals MVP").

A new era started in 2022 when previous assistant [Andrzej Urban](/wiki/Andrzej_Urban "Andrzej Urban") was promoted to head coach. The team management reoriented its roster composition philosophy, focusing more on European foreigners. With players like [Aigars Šķēle](/wiki/Aigars_%C5%A0%C4%B7%C4%93le "Aigars Šķēle"), [Ojārs Siliņš](/wiki/Oj%C4%81rs_Sili%C5%86%C5%A1 "Ojārs Siliņš") and [Nemanja Đurišić](/wiki/Nemanja_%C4%90uri%C5%A1i%C4%87 "Nemanja Đurišić"), Stal hit the mark and won its first [Polish Supercup](/wiki/Polish_Basketball_Supercup "Polish Basketball Supercup"). By winning the [European North Basketball League](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_European_North_Basketball_League "2022–23 European North Basketball League"), Stal won its first international title. Urban's debut season ended with bronze in the [Polish league](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_PLK_season "2022–23 PLK season"). Before his second season with the team, Latvian international Šķēle became the first ever current state Stal Ostrów Wielkopolski player in the [FIBA Basketball World Cup](/wiki/2023_FIBA_Basketball_World_Cup "2023 FIBA Basketball World Cup").

## Arenas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stal_Ostr%C3%B3w_Wielkopolski&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Arenas")]

For most of its existence, the team played in the Hala Sportowa Stal, which had a capacity of 1,800 people. Since 2020, the club plays its home games in the [Arena Ostrów](/wiki/Arena_Ostr%C3%B3w "Arena Ostrów"), which has capacity for 3,000 people.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The arena cost 31 million [PLN](/wiki/Polish_z%C5%82oty "Polish złoty") and was built by local company Bud-Rem.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
